steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: The GCF of $12$ and $27$ is $3$. Factor it out:$$12x+27=3(4x+9)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $3(4x+9)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. Each term in the given expression, $12x+27$, has a common factor of $3$. Therefore, the expression can be rewritten as $3\left(4x\right)+3\left(9\right)$, or $3\left(4x+9\right)$. Thus, the expression $3\left(4x+9\right)$ is equivalent to the given expression.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $108x+12$, not $12x+27$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $324x+27$, not $12x+27$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $27x+72$, not $12x+27$.
